channel.title: Gravel Racing on Mizubikes
channel.description: Recent content in Gravel Racing on Mizubikes

item.title: Crux 5 vs Road Bike: Is the Quiver Killer Finally Here?
item.description: The Crux 5 weighs 789g, clears 55mm tires, and borrows aero shaping from the Tarmac SL8. Here's the honest case for replacing your road bike with one.

item.title: Specialized Crux 5: The Gravel Race Bike Goes Full Aero
item.description: Specialized officially launches the Crux 5 with aero tube shaping, 55mm tire clearance, and a FACT 12r frame under 790 grams.

item.title: The Crux 5 Is the Gravel Bike We Knew Was Coming
item.description: The Specialized Crux 5 goes full aero and leaves its cyclocross roots behind. Here's why that was inevitable — and what it might cost us down the road.

item.title: 32-Inch Wheels Make More Sense for Gravel Than XC — And Scott Just Proved It
item.description: Scott's 32-inch gravel prototype headed to Unbound makes a compelling case that bigger wheels belong in gravel racing before they ever belong in XC.

item.title: ENVE G SES Gravel Wheels: The 4.5 Pro and 6.7 Pro Go Wider Than Ever
item.description: ENVE's new G SES gravel wheels push rim widths to 35mm internal on the flagship 6.7 Pro. Here's what separates the three models and who they're actually for.

item.title: ENVE G SES vs. Zipp 303 XPLR: Aero Gravel Wheels Compared
item.description: ENVE G SES 4.5, 4.5 Pro, and 6.7 Pro vs. Zipp 303 XPLR S and SW — specs, pricing, internal width, and who each wheel is actually for.
